part.text:
# Next.js API Route Proxy for Prembly

## Background
Create a Next.js API route that acts as a secure proxy to the Prembly NIN verification API, hiding the API key from the frontend.

## Requirements
- Initialize a Next.js project in `/home/user/app`.
- Create an API route `POST /api/verify-nin` that accepts a JSON body with a `number` field.
- The route should call the Prembly NIN verification API (`https://api.prembly.com/api/v1/verification/nin` or `https://api.prembly.com/verification/nin` according to the documentation) using the `PREMBLY_APP_ID` and `PREMBLY_API_KEY` environment variables.
- Set the headers `app-id` and `x-api-key` appropriately.
- Return the JSON response from Prembly to the client.
- The API route must handle errors appropriately.

## Implementation Guide
1. Initialize a Next.js project in `/home/user/app` using `npx create-next-app@latest app --javascript --eslint --tailwind --app --src-dir --use-npm`.
2. Create the API route in `src/app/api/verify-nin/route.js`.
3. Implement the POST handler to read the `number` from the request body.
4. Make a POST request to the Prembly sandbox API using `fetch` or `axios`.
5. Return the response to the client.

## Constraints
- Project path: `/home/user/app`
- Start command: `npm run build && npm start`
- Port: 3000
- Use `https://api.prembly.com` as the base URL for the Prembly API.

## Integrations
- Prembly

# Next.js API Route Proxy for Prembly

## Background
Create a Next.js API route that acts as a secure proxy to the Prembly NIN verification API, hiding the API key from the frontend.

## Requirements
- Initialize a Next.js project in `/home/user/app`.
- Create an API route `POST /api/verify-nin` that accepts a JSON body with a `number` field.
- The route should call the Prembly NIN verification API (`https://api.prembly.com/api/v1/verification/nin` or `https://api.prembly.com/verification/nin` according to the documentation) using the `PREMBLY_APP_ID` and `PREMBLY_API_KEY` environment variables.
- Set the headers `app-id` and `x-api-key` appropriately.
- Return the JSON response from Prembly to the client.
- The API route must handle errors appropriately.

## Implementation Guide
1. Initialize a Next.js project in `/home/user/app` using `npx create-next-app@latest app --javascript --eslint --tailwind --app --src-dir --use-npm`.
2. Create the API route in `src/app/api/verify-nin/route.js`.
3. Implement the POST handler to read the `number` from the request body.
4. Make a POST request to the Prembly sandbox API using `fetch` or `axios`.
5. Return the response to the client.

## Constraints
- Project path: `/home/user/app`
- Start command: `npm run build && npm start`
- Port: 3000
- Use `https://api.prembly.com` as the base URL for the Prembly API.

## Integrations
- Prembly

content.text:
# NIN - Authentication
created: 2023-03-21, last updated: 2026-04-17, [Read more](https://docs.prembly.com/docs/nin-and-virtual-nin-copy)

The verification workflow for a virtual NIN can be performed using either of these processes:
**Using NIMC App: **
1. The user inputs Identitypass's Enterprise code or ID (696739) on the NIMC mobile app
2. The user scans the merchant QR code on the NIMC
3. NIMC returns virtual NIN to the user on the NIMC mobile app
4. User inputs virtual NIN on merchant's app
…
> ##
> POST
> https://api.prembly.com/verification/vnin
**Request Header**
...
**Request Body**
…
|Response|Description|
|--|--|
|residencestatus|The residency status of the individual.|
|pmiddlename|The middle name of the individual's parent.|
|email|The email address of the individual.|
|nok_postalcode|The postal code of the individual's next of kin.|
|nin|The national identification number of the individual.|
|employmentstatus|The employment status of the individual.|
|birthlga|The local government area of the individual's birthplace.|
|base64Image|The person's image encoded in base64 format.|
|verification|Provides details about the verification process itself.|
|status|Indicates the status of the verification.|

# National ID Basic - Authentication
created: 2023-03-21, last updated: 2026-04-15, [Read more](https://docs.prembly.com/docs/national-id-basic-1)

### Sample Response
```
{
"detail": "NIN Verification Successful",
"response_code": "00",
"status": true,
"nin_data": {
"birthdate": "dd-mm-yyyy",
"firstname": "firstname",
"gender": "m",
"middlename": "middlename",
"surname": "surname",
"telephoneno": "08012345678",
"residence_address": "Sample address",
"photo": "base64Image String"
},
"verification": {
"status": "VERIFIED",
"reference": "645",
"verification_id": 645
},
"widget_info": {},
"session": {
"_auth_user_id": "1",
"_auth_user_backend": "django.contrib.auth.backends.ModelBackend",
"_auth_user_hash": "e0b35162cdca1d24e4dff1c05dd6ac2ea182e4945acab549e71d705d02a12f76"
},
"endpoint_name": "NIN BASIC"
}
```

# GitHub - prembly/prembly_python
created: 2023-03-05, last updated: 2025-05-18, [Read more](https://github.com/prembly/prembly_python)

# Prembly Identitypass PyPI version (prembly_python)
The Python library for the Prembly Identitypass API.The Python library provides easy access to Prembly Identitypass API's from Django, Flask, and other Python apps.
It abstracts the complexity involved in direct integration and allows you to make quick calls to the APIs.

# How to Test APIs in the Prembly Sandbox | Step-by-Step Guide
created: 2025-09-23, last updated: 2025-10-02, [Read more](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YJVTpvNXAMk)

In this video, we’ll show you how to:
✔️ Access the API documentation
✔️ Generate your Sandbox API Keys and App ID
✔️ Run test calls with mock data
✔️ Review sandbox responses
✔️ Switch from test to live mode safely
…
...
{ts:0} How to run a test on the Prembley Sandbox environment.
{ts:4} Step one, open the API documentation.
Go to docs.premblely.com.
Click API reference at the top.
Select
{ts:14} your country, for example, Nigeria, and choose an endpoint.
Step two, log in to the dashboard.
Go to
{ts:27} integrations API.
Navigate to applications.
Create a new app ID.
Copy the app ID you created.
{ts:38} Paste the app ID into the Docs portal.
Go back to the dashboard.
Click API keys.
Switch to test/ sandbox mode.

## How To Regenerate Your Live API Keys
- On the side menu under the Integrations section, click API library.- Under API keys.- Click the toggle button to switch between Sandbox and Live keys.- Ensure what is displayed on the screen is “Regenerate Live Keys”- Click “Regenerate Live key
identitypass.prembly.com 12How To Regenerate Your Sandbox API Keys- On the side menu under the Integrations section, click API library.- Click API keys.- Click the toggle button to switch between Sandbox and Live keys.- Ensure what is displayed on the screen is “Regenerate Sandbox Keys” as shown below.- Click on “Regenerate Sandbox key”

input.result:
I have successfully initialized the Next.js project and implemented the secure API proxy for Prembly NIN verification.

### Accomplishments:
1.  **Project Initialization**: Created a new Next.js application in `/home/user/app` with the specified configurations (JavaScript, ESLint, Tailwind CSS, App Router, and src directory).
2.  **API Route Implementation**: Developed the `POST /api/verify-nin` route in `src/app/api/verify-nin/route.js`.
    -   Extracts the `number` from the request body.
    -   Securely retrieves `PREMBLY_APP_ID` and `PREMBLY_API_KEY` from environment variables.
    -   Proxies the request to `https://api.prembly.com/api/v1/verification/nin` with the required `app-id` and `x-api-key` headers.
    -   Handles errors gracefully and returns the Prembly API response to the client.
3.  **Environment Configuration**: Created a `.env.local.example` and a `.env.local` template for the necessary API credentials.

### Project Details:
-   **Path**: `/home/user/app`
